deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras -n oras --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue." + }, + { + "title": "Review oras configuration", + "description": "Inspect the relevant oras configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras\nkubectl get configmap -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oras\n```\n### What happened in your environment?\n\n1) Created a package containing a symbolic link:\n\n\n\n2) Pushed it to my OCI complaint" + }, + { + "title": "Apply the fix for Can't download an OCI package twice if package contains…", + "description": "Bump the `oras-go` dependency to the latest commit [`cb6d75be7dd4a78f25fe9d00c76c0d15c4296da6`](https://github.com/oras-project/oras-go/commit/cb6d75be7dd4a78f25fe9d00c76c0d15c4296da6) which includes the bug fix for https://github.com/oras-project/oras-go/issues/865.\n\n*(optional, in `fixes #\n\n4) Without cleaning anything in the output folder, I tried the same command from (3) again, and then it failed:\n```" -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras -n oras --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n oras --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone." + } + ], + "resolution":
